title: Room 1: Grammar Library
description: Stacks of books whisper clues. A sentence waits for the correct part of speech.
question: In the sentence 'She quickly ran home,' what part of speech is 'quickly'?
success: The books hush, and the shelf-door opens.

title: Room 2: Synonym Hall
description: Portraits line the hall. One asks for a word closest in meaning to 'brave.'
question: Which word is the best synonym for 'brave'?
success: The portraits nod and the next passage unlocks.

title: Room 3: Reading Chamber
description: A paragraph glows on the wall. The room asks what the author's main point is called.
question: What do we call the central point of a paragraph or passage?
hint: It is the big idea the text supports.
success: The glowing text fades into an open doorway.
